geographic_scope: Shatila refugee camp, Sabra neighborhood, and immediately adjoining blocks in West Beirut
theater_description: A tightly bounded camp-and-neighborhood massacre conducted during 16-18 September 1982 inside an area encircled by Israeli forces and entered by Lebanese Forces/Phalangist militiamen.
geometry_method_note: Footprint is camp-centered but includes the adjacent Sabra urban area because the source base itself describes killings in the camps and immediate surrounding area.
military_area_note: The core victim pool is overwhelmingly described as civilians, but the current bundle does not treat the full death total as cleanly status-coded because the broader literature and some lists do not fully reconcile all statuses.
civilian_area_note: Administrative camp counts and local wider-area population estimates are too heterogeneous to serve as a publishable massacre-window civilian person-month denominator.
split_reason: One episode is preferred because the direct-death issue is stronger than any meaningful internal temporal split within the 16-18 September massacre window.
quality_note: The massacre is tightly bounded and richly sourced, but the death toll is contested and denominator evidence for the population present is poor.
